title: China-to-Colombia BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 60 Hz Electrical Design and Grid Settings
rows.target_requirement: Colombia BESS export configurations should be validated for the local 60 Hz electricity system and for the frequency, voltage, protection, metering, and communications settings required by the interconnection route. PCS, transformers, relays, HVAC, auxiliary power supplies, pumps, fire equipment, meters, and controls should be checked against the actual Colombian project grid conditions before shipment and commissioning.
target_standards: RETIE electrical installation evidence where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic grid-storage equipment and factory files are commonly prepared around 50 Hz assumptions. China GB/T storage documentation can support system design, but the export configuration should separately prove 60 Hz ratings, control settings, protection behavior, and auxiliary-load compatibility.
rows.gap: Identify every 50 Hz-only component or setting in the China design file. Provide 60 Hz nameplate evidence, PCS type-test settings, relay settings, transformer and auxiliary-load ratings, and updated FAT / SAT procedures for Colombia.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as an engineering and interconnection requirement where Colombia 60 Hz grid connection, equipment safety, or commissioning requirements apply
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic evidence may be 50 Hz-based unless the export configuration is specifically tested and rated for 60 Hz

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Colombia export configurations should be proven at 60 Hz. A China 50 Hz grid-storage file is not enough unless the actual PCS and balance-of-system configuration is rated, tested, and set for Colombia.

source.publisher: Unidad de Planeacion Minero Energetica (UPME), Colombia

rows.requirement: China GB 44240-2024 / GB/T 36276-2023 Versus Colombia Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: Colombia reviewers may use China GB evidence as supporting technical documentation, but acceptance depends on RETIE scope, recognized technical standards, conformity-assessment route, project specifications, grid studies, fire review, and transport documentation. GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 should be positioned as China-origin evidence, not automatic Colombia market-access approvals.
rows.china_equivalent: GB 44240-2024 addresses safety requirements for lithium cells and batteries used in electrical energy storage systems. GB/T 36276-2023 addresses lithium-ion batteries for electrical energy storage. Both are relevant to BESS exports from China but must be mapped to Colombia product, installation, transport, fire, grid, and project requirements.
rows.gap: Create a clause crosswalk from GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 to requested RETIE, IEC, CREG, grid, transport, fire, and project clauses. Identify missing Spanish documentation, local labels, recognized-standard evidence, model files, site fire evidence, and Colombia-specific declarations.

mandatory_status.target: Not a Colombia mandatory standard by itself unless required by RETIE recognition, contract, permit, buyer, lender, insurer, grid operator, or technical specification
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where its Chinese scope applies; GB/T 36276-2023 is recommended unless made mandatory by procurement, project approval, or another instrument

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 are useful China-origin BESS evidence, but they are not Colombia approvals. Use them in a documented crosswalk against the Colombia project route.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation Standard Public Service Platform, China

rows.target_requirement: Colombia fire-safety obligations are site and authority dependent. Law 1575 of 2012 establishes the national fire-service framework, while BESS projects may also need municipal, building, insurer, workplace-safety, emergency-response, and project-owner review. RETIE electrical compliance and battery safety reports do not by themselves close thermal-runaway, separation, ventilation, gas detection, fire suppression, access, water supply, and emergency-response expectations.
target_standards: Project fire-safety design and emergency-response requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China BESS projects commonly reference GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, GB/T 36558-2023, container fire designs, and local Chinese fire review documents. These can support due diligence but do not replace Colombian local fire-authority, insurer, emergency-response, or owner acceptance.
rows.gap: Prepare a Spanish fire-safety package with site layout, separation distances, ventilation, gas detection, fire detection and suppression logic, emergency shutdown, access routes, water or agent assumptions, thermal-runaway mitigation evidence, SDS, and emergency response procedures.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where local fire, building, workplace-safety, municipal, insurer, or permit review applies; detailed BESS tests and design criteria are project-specific unless expressly incorporated
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese project approval, installation location, and local fire review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Fire review is a separate Colombia project workstream. Battery test reports and RETIE files should be supplemented with site-specific fire and emergency-response evidence.

source.publisher: Departamento Administrativo de la Funcion Publica, Colombia

rows.requirement: CREG Storage and Grid Connection Treatment
rows.target_requirement: Grid-connected BESS in Colombia should be reviewed under the applicable CREG electricity-market, connection, dispatch, and network rules for the project route. CREG Resolucion 098 de 2019 created a regulatory route for electrical energy storage systems used to mitigate network constraints, but it should not be read as a universal product certificate for every BESS import. Project owners should confirm whether the BESS is generation-linked, stand-alone, behind-the-meter, network-support, ancillary-service, or isolated-system storage.
target_standards: Applicable CREG electricity-market and grid-connection rules
Project-specific grid studies and operator requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-storage evidence such as GB/T 36558-2023, GB/T 36276-2023, PCS model data, relay studies, and dispatch-interface documentation can support technical due diligence. It does not replace Colombia-specific CREG classification, connection approval, network studies, market registration, telemetry, dispatch, or operator testing.
rows.gap: Prepare Colombia-specific grid models, control descriptions, active/reactive power capability, protection settings, frequency and voltage ride-through assumptions, telemetry lists, commissioning tests, and market or operator interface documents for the chosen storage use case.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected projects where CREG, network-operator, market, dispatch, or interconnection rules apply; project-specific for behind-the-meter and isolated systems
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese grid connection, project approval, and dispatch requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] CREG and grid treatment is route-dependent. A China battery or IEC report does not close Colombia connection, dispatch, market, or operator testing requirements.

source.publisher: Comision de Regulacion de Energia y Gas (CREG), Colombia

rows.requirement: IEC 62619 / IEC 62933 Evidence for Colombia Projects
rows.target_requirement: IEC 62619 is an international safety standard for secondary lithium cells and batteries for industrial applications, and IEC 62933 is the IEC series for electrical energy storage systems. In Colombia, these standards should be treated as project, buyer, lender, insurer, engineering, or RETIE-equivalence evidence unless a Colombian law, RETIE decision, tender, grid requirement, or contract expressly makes a specific clause or edition mandatory.
target_standards: RETIE recognition or equivalence route where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 are highly relevant China-origin BESS battery standards, but they are not one-for-one substitutes for IEC 62619 or IEC 62933 evidence when a Colombian project document asks for IEC clauses, system safety documentation, functional safety assumptions, or grid-storage performance evidence.
rows.gap: Map China cell, module, rack, BMS, PCS, EMS, enclosure, HVAC, and fire-mitigation evidence to the exact IEC clauses requested by the Colombia buyer, lender, engineer, RETIE reviewer, or grid study. Confirm editions before relying on bare standard numbers.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary or project-specific unless incorporated by RETIE, a Colombian authority, grid requirement, tender, contract, lender requirement, or insurer requirement
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where its Chinese scope applies; GB/T 36276-2023 is recommended unless made mandatory by procurement, project approval, or another instrument

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC evidence may be commercially important in Colombia BESS projects, but mandatory status depends on the specific Colombian instrument or contract. Do not rely on China GB reports without an IEC crosswalk.

rows.requirement: RETIE Electrical Product and Installation Scope
rows.target_requirement: Colombia's current RETIE was issued through Resolucion 40117 of 2 April 2024, with later transitional amendments including Resolucion 40304 of 2025. BESS projects should screen batteries, inverters or PCS, controllers, switchgear, transformers, prefabricated rooms, cabling, protection, and the complete electrical installation against RETIE product, installation, and conformity-assessment books. MinEnergia's RETIE FAQ also identifies batteries and regulators or controllers for batteries used in solar, wind, or grid-injection accumulation systems among products that may use a transitional supplier declaration route.
rows.china_equivalent: China BESS documentation commonly relies on GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, GB/T 36558-2023, and Chinese installation or project acceptance files. These can support technical review, but they do not determine RETIE product scope, Colombian conformity-assessment route, installation declarations, or inspection duties.
rows.gap: Prepare a RETIE scope matrix for each imported component and for the site installation. Include Spanish technical files, nameplates, single-line diagrams, installation declarations, conformity documents, supplier declarations where the transitional route is valid, and inspection evidence where required.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where RETIE product, installation, or conformity-assessment scope applies; transitional supplier declaration is route-specific and time-sensitive
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where its Chinese scope applies; GB/T 36276-2023 and GB/T 36558-2023 are recommended unless made mandatory by procurement, project approval, or another instrument

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] RETIE is the first Colombia-specific screen for BESS electrical products and installations. China GB reports should be packaged as supporting evidence, not as a substitute for RETIE scope and conformity decisions.

source.publisher: Ministerio de Minas y Energia, Colombia

rows.target_requirement: Lithium cells and batteries shipped to Colombia normally need dangerous-goods transport classification and test evidence aligned with UN Manual of Tests and Criteria section 38.3, then applied through the relevant air, sea, road, carrier, port, customs, and importer documentation route. UN 38.3 is transport evidence; it is not a Colombian RETIE approval, fire approval, or grid-connection approval.
target_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, section 38.3
Applicable modal dangerous-goods rules for shipment to Colombia
Carrier and importer dangerous-goods documentation requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China export files often include UN 38.3 test reports, MSDS or SDS, battery test summaries, packaging evidence, and dangerous-goods declarations. These remain necessary for transport, but they do not replace Colombian electrical, fire, or grid project approvals.
rows.gap: Keep transport evidence tied to the exact cell, module, pack, and container configuration being shipped. Recheck whether changed cell suppliers, pack layouts, SOC limits, packaging, or containerized integration require updated test summaries or carrier acceptance.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for regulated lithium-battery dangerous-goods transport where applicable; not a substitute for Colombia market, electrical, fire, or grid approvals
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for export transport where lithium battery dangerous-goods rules apply

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] UN 38.3 is a transport gate, not a Colombia BESS market-access approval. Treat it as shipment evidence and keep separate RETIE, fire, and grid approval files.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
